source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Crane/Lifting Device

event.incident_type: Structural damage to lifting device with personnel injury
actions_taken: All operations stopped immediately following incident
Medic examined injured person on scene
Injured person sent for further evaluation after consulting with onshore physician
Broken Pipe Stop Arm sent for third-party metallurgical testing
Metallurgical and failure analysis testing performed by Howard and Associates
event.summary: On September 25, 2014, at 1350 hours, an employee working on the deck of Weatherford's Hydraulic Pulling Unit #4 was struck on the right arm by a Pipe Stop Arm (PSA) that fell 24 feet from the derrick. The PSA, weighing approximately 165 pounds and measuring 8 feet in length, broke off during the process of lowering it while racking back pipe from the A004 well. The injured person sustained no fractures or broken bones and was released to full duty the following day. Root cause analysis identified incomplete weld joint penetration and shallow/thin welding along the fractured weld, combined with overload conditions (tension, bending, and/or torsional).
recommendations: Design documentation should specify that Complete Joint Penetration (CJP) welds are required for Pipe Stop Arms

threats.name: Incomplete weld joint penetration in Pipe Stop Arm
Overload conditions on Pipe Stop Arm (tension, bending, torsional)

controls.name: Design specification for Complete Joint Penetration welds
controls.barrier_role: Prevent defective equipment design and manufacture
controls.lod_basis: Design control to prevent manufacture of defective equipment

supporting_text: Design Documentation failed to specify that Complete Joint Penetration (CJP) welds were required for the 'Pipe Stop Arm.'

controls.name: Weld quality control and inspection during manufacturing
controls.barrier_role: Detect and prevent defective welds before equipment deployment
controls.lod_basis: Manufacturing quality assurance to prevent defective welds

supporting_text: full penetration had not been achieved around the circumference of the weld, and areas of shallow/thin weld were observed along the fractured weld
Lap testing of the PSA showed a lack of fusion in the intact welds

controls.name: Load analysis and equipment design verification
controls.barrier_role: Ensure equipment is designed to withstand operational loads
controls.lod_basis: Design verification to prevent overload failure

supporting_text: the fracture occurred due to overload (such as tension, bending, and/or torsional overload)

controls.name: Work area exclusion and personnel positioning during lifting operations
controls.barrier_role: Prevent personnel exposure to falling object hazard
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to keep personnel out of hazard zone

supporting_text: A Weatherford employee working on the deck below the PSA was struck on his right arm by the falling debris

controls.name: Personal Protective Equipment (hard hat, arm protection)
controls.barrier_role: Reduce severity of injury from falling object impact

supporting_text: The IP sustained no fractures or broken bones and was released to full duty the following day

controls.name: Immediate incident response and medical evaluation
controls.barrier_role: Minimize injury consequences through rapid medical intervention
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response to mitigate injury outcomes

supporting_text: All operations were stopped immediately following the incident, and the medic arrived on the scene to examine the Injured Person (IP). After consulting with an onshore physician, the decision was made to send the IP in for further evaluation.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
